items.reason: All 20 decision areas in `docs/decisions/decisions-needed.md` map to DEC-001 through DEC-020 in `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md`, each with id, question, options_considered, choice, consequences, and projects_to populated (e.g., DEC-016 through DEC-020 each list 3 options, a concrete choice, multi-bullet consequences, and 4+ projects_to artifacts).

`docs/decisions/decisions-pending.md` lists PEND-001 (target 2026-05-15) and PEND-005 (target 2026-05-03), each with owner Andy, named blocker, description, and rationale. Research date is 2026-04-24, so target dates are still in the future.

Each decision's consequences section names concrete artifacts removed: DEC-001 'Removes per-function subject-to-person merge branches from supabase/functions/lti-launch-handler/index.ts...', DEC-016 'Removes direct edits to dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json... as an accepted workflow', DEC-019 'Removes depends_on_entity and depends_on_contents handling from scripts/generate_caliper_core.py'. 'Why not collapse' justifications appear on DEC-001 through DEC-014.

Each decision lists three credible alternatives bracketing the design space: DEC-016 considers 'commit only shared dictionary and generate on demand' (aggressive) vs 'keep per-spec canonical' (permissive); DEC-005 considers AGS-as-canonical vs peer source-native vs current-state model; DEC-009 considers QTI-as-canonical vs separate catalogs vs broad resource concept. None are paper-tiger options.

DEC-016 declares `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` upstream and per-spec files generated, but no `scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py` exists. VERIFY.md only runs per-spec generators (`generate_oneroster_core.py`, etc.) that read FROM `dictionary/*.v1.json` as the source, not from the shared seed. `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py` validates per-spec dictionaries against generated SQL/OpenAPI/docs, never against the shared seed. The per-spec files are still hand-edited, which DEC-016 says should be a build error.
items.traced_cause: scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py: create the generator that reads data/data-dictionary.seed.json and writes dictionary/*.v1.json; expand data/data-dictionary.seed.json to a canonical model with every projected object, field, enum, and privacy class; add CI check (in check_dictionary_artifacts.py or a new script) that fails when generator output diverges from committed per-spec files.

items.reason: DEC-017 requires every overlapping spec field to carry `canonical_field_id`, but no per-spec field in evidence carries that key. `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person.email`, `dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json#caliper_actor.identifier_value`, and `dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json#lti_membership.email` each carry only `sourceStandard` and `decision_id`, not a `canonical_field_id` pointing to a shared canonical email field. The overlap is resolved only in DEC-001/DEC-017 prose.
items.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json: add canonical email/identity fields under a shared object; dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person.email, dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json#caliper_actor.identifier_value, dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json#lti_membership.email: add canonical_field_id pointing to the shared email field; extend scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py to require canonical_field_id or spec_only on every cross-spec field.

items.reason: No identity-shaped object in evidence carries `canonical_object_id`. `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person`, `dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json#caliper_actor`, and `dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json#lti_membership` each carry only `sourceStandard` at the object level. There is no canonical identity entity record in `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` that the per-spec objects project to. DEC-001 unification exists only as prose.
items.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json: add a canonical identity entity (with fields, allowed values, projections); dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person, dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json#caliper_actor, dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json#lti_membership, dictionary/qti-core.v1.json (Candidate when added): add canonical_object_id pointing to that entity.

items.reason: DEC-018 requires `shared_enum_id` references with bidirectional spec-native crosswalks. Per-spec dictionaries currently use `allowed_values_ref` pointing to a per-file `shared_allowed_values` block (e.g., `base_status`, `system_identifier_type` inside oneroster-core.v1.json), not a global shared enum. There is no cross-dictionary mapping showing OneRoster `student`, LTI `Learner`, and Caliper `Learner` mapping to the same canonical role.
items.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json: add a shared_enums section with role_family, status, identifier_type, etc., each listing canonical values plus spec-native crosswalks; dictionary/*.v1.json: replace allowed_values/allowed_values_ref with shared_enum_id; update scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py to require bidirectional crosswalks.

items.reason: DEC-019 explicitly forbids `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents`, but `dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json` still uses both: `caliper_entity.name` has `"privacy_class": "depends_on_entity"`, `caliper_entity.description` has `"privacy_class": "depends_on_entity"`, and `caliper_entity.extensions` has `"privacy_class": "depends_on_contents"`. The decision was made but not projected into the data.
items.traced_cause: dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json#caliper_entity.name, .description, .extensions, and any other depends_on_* occurrences: replace each with one concrete class from DEC-019's closed list (public, operational, directory, education_record, behavioral, sensitive, credential, privacy_governance, system); add a check in scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py that fails when privacy_class is not in the closed list.

items.reason: DEC-020 declares relationships should live in the dictionary and migrations should be generated, but `supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql` is hand-written with explicit `references public.classes`, `references public.people`, and check constraints. Dictionary objects in evidence (e.g., `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#class`) do not carry a `relationships` block declaring FK targets and cardinality. No generator produces the SQL migration from the dictionary.
items.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json and dictionary/*.v1.json: add a relationships block on each object declaring source_field, target_object, target_field, cardinality, ownership, on_delete; create scripts/generate_supabase_migrations.py that emits supabase/migrations/*.sql from that graph; add VERIFY step that fails when generator output diverges.

items.reason: Every per-spec field carries a `decision_id` and `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py` validates citations against the decision register's machine-readable trace. However, objects themselves carry only `sourceStandard` (no object-level `decision_id`), and shared_allowed_values entries carry only `sourceStandard` per value, not a `decision_id`. Privacy classes are used as inline strings rather than as enumerated entities with their own decision_id citations.
items.traced_cause: dictionary/*.v1.json: add decision_id to every object record, every shared_allowed_values value, and a top-level privacy_classes block citing DEC-011 / DEC-019; extend scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py to require decision_id at object, enum-value, and privacy-class levels.

items.reason: Generated dictionary docs exist for OneRoster, QTI, CASE, Caliper, and integration/governance (`docs/generated/*-dictionary.md` and `site/docs/*-dictionary.html`), and `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py` validates per-field markdown/HTML coverage. But hand-edited prose pages like `docs/lead-spec-accounting.md` make load-bearing field claims (e.g., listing every Demographics field name) without anchored links into the dictionary. README and supabase-hosted-database.md describe field shapes in prose without dictionary anchors. Dictionary changes would not auto-regenerate these prose pages.
items.traced_cause: scripts/build_site_docs.py and scripts/generate_*.py: emit per-object/field anchor pages that prose docs can link to; rewrite docs/lead-spec-accounting.md, docs/dictionary-coverage-matrix.md, and README field references to link to those anchors; add a check that fails when prose names a field the dictionary does not have.

items.reason: The decision register renders as HTML at `site/docs/decisions-standards-overlap-decisions.html` with anchors per decision, and `site/index.html` has a Decisions section that renders DEC cards from the markdown. However, concept-level docs do not consistently link to decisions: `docs/lead-spec-accounting.md` discusses identity/agent without linking to DEC-001's anchor in plain language, and `docs/dictionary-coverage-matrix.md` introduces tenant-owned vs shared reference data without referencing DEC-010. The 'why' is largely confined to the decision register file itself rather than in the docs that describe the concepts.
items.traced_cause: docs/lead-spec-accounting.md, docs/dictionary-coverage-matrix.md, and README.md: add plain-language 'why' callouts at each concept that link to the anchored decision (e.g., '#dec-001-person-agent-subject') and summarize the simplification the decision produced.

items.reason: No `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` (or equivalent end-to-end teaching app guide) exists in the evidence. The README and `docs/supabase-hosted-database.md` show isolated curl examples for the gradebook bulk-submit and audited-roster-read Edge Functions, but there is no walkthrough of: (a) creating a school org with one teacher and ten students, (b) creating a class with those students enrolled, (c) posting a numeric grade, (d) linking that assignment to a specific CASE standard URI, or (e) reading a Caliper-style activity feed for the class.
items.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: write the end-to-end guide with five copy-pasteable sections, each linking to dictionary anchors and global enum entries. Steps (d) and (e) also require runtime decisions: link CASE alignment writes to a decision (or document the SQL/REST workaround), and define a Caliper read endpoint or document the audit_log read pattern as the activity feed.

items.reason: `site/docs/index.html` links every rendered doc page within reach of the portal, and `site/index.html` has a sitemap-style nav. But there is no link-checker output committed to evidence, so 404s/anchor breakage cannot be verified. `docs/eval-rubric.md` and `docs/admin-operations.md` are reachable, but the buildability guide referenced in this rubric (`docs/build-an-edtech-app.md`) does not exist, so any forward link to it would 404.
items.traced_cause: scripts/check_doc_links.py (new): walk the rendered site, follow every <a href> and dictionary anchor, fail on 404 or missing anchor; commit the most recent report to site/api/link-check.json; add to VERIFY.md.

items.reason: A layperson reading only docs/ cannot produce executable artifacts for the five required steps. Step (a) creating a school: docs/supabase-hosted-database.md describes the schema but provides no curl/SQL for inserting a new org+teacher+10 students under their own tenant; the schema is read-only via PostgREST. Step (b) creating a class with enrollments: no documented endpoint or SQL recipe. Step (c) posting a grade: gradebook-bulk-submit curl exists but field semantics like sourcedId vs internal id are not unified across docs. Step (d) linking to a CASE standard URI: DEC-012 says CASE is doc-only, so there is no runtime path. Step (e) Caliper feed: only an ingestion endpoint exists; there is no documented read-back feed for a class.
items.traced_cause: Multi-layer: write docs/build-an-edtech-app.md with concrete curl/SQL for steps (a)-(e); add canonical_field_id resolution to dictionary so the guide can link to one canonical email/role field; either build runtime slices for CASE alignment writes and a Caliper class feed, or document a deterministic SQL workaround in the guide and update DEC-012 to declare those workarounds.

items.reason: `scripts/evaluate_platform.py` enforces traced_cause with `ensure_traced_causes()`, `normalize_traced_cause()`, and a per-rubric-id `DEFAULT_TRACED_CAUSES` map; it also normalizes buildability_gaps with traced_cause via `normalize_buildability_gap()` and `normalize_gap_trace()`. Each non-pass item in the JSON output is required to have a non-empty traced_cause naming the lowest-layer artifact and required change.

VERIFY.md ends with `python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json`, executed by the loop after every iteration via `read_verify_commands()` in scripts/codex_loop.py. The script writes counts, done flag, items with traced_cause, and buildability_gaps to site/api/platform-evaluation.json (per `main()` in evaluate_platform.py).

`scripts/codex_loop.py` has `read_overrides()` which reads docs/loop-overrides.md, `overrides_paused()` which checks for a PAUSE token on its own line outside HTML comments, and `format_overrides_for_prompt()` which injects the cleaned text under a 'Human overrides' section in `build_prompt()`. The PAUSE check runs at the top of each iteration before Codex is invoked, halting before the next iteration runs.

`scripts/codex_loop.py` calls `run_evaluator()` and returns `0` when `after_eval.get('done')` is true, and again checks `before_eval.get('done')` at the start of each iteration. The COMPLETE marker from Codex is only respected when the evaluator agrees ('Codex reported COMPLETE but evaluator says work remains. Continuing.'). No numeric-threshold gate is used for termination.

buildability_gaps.gap: No documented curl or SQL for creating a new school org with one teacher and ten students under a fresh tenant. docs/supabase-hosted-database.md only documents reading seeded data; the public REST policy is read-only on organizations/people, and no Edge Function exposes tenant-scoped writes for orgs.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md (new): show the SQL or admin endpoint a layperson would use, including how to obtain a tenant-scoped JWT; if no public write path exists, add a `roster-bootstrap` Edge Function and document it. Update supabase/migrations to allow tenant-scoped INSERTs on organizations/people.

buildability_gaps.gap: No documented endpoint or SQL for creating a class with enrollments. The seed contains classes/enrollments, but there is no documented write path; a layperson cannot determine whether to POST to PostgREST, call an Edge Function, or run SQL.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: document the class creation path with copy-pasteable curl. Add tenant-scoped write policies to supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql for classes and enrollments, or expose a `class-create` Edge Function.

buildability_gaps.gap: gradebook-bulk-submit curl exists, but the docs do not link `sourcedId`, `lineItemId`, `personId`, and `scoreStatus` to the canonical dictionary fields, nor explain how `sourcedId` differs from `id`. Layperson must read the Edge Function source to understand semantics.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: include the full curl plus links to dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#result.score, #result.sourced_id, etc., plus a plain-English mapping table. Add canonical_field_id (DEC-017) so the guide references one canonical learner identifier across specs.

buildability_gaps.gap: No runtime exists to link an assignment to a CASE standard URI, and no documented SQL/JSON shape for storing the alignment. DEC-012 says CASE is doc-only.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: Either (a) build a CASE alignment runtime slice (new Edge Function plus supabase/migrations table for assignment_case_alignments) and document it in docs/build-an-edtech-app.md, or (b) update DEC-012 with a documented SQL workaround (e.g., write CASE URIs into a generic line_items.standard_uris JSONB column) and document that workaround in the guide.

buildability_gaps.gap: Only the caliper-event-ingestion endpoint is documented; there is no read API for a Caliper-style activity feed for a class. Layperson cannot retrieve events the way the rubric requires.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: Either (a) add a `caliper-feed` Edge Function that returns tenant-scoped events filtered by group_id/class_id, with curl in docs/build-an-edtech-app.md and a dictionary entry for the feed shape, or (b) document a tenant-scoped PostgREST read against caliper_event projections (which currently do not exist as runtime tables) and add the corresponding migration.

projections.note: Structural per-decision projection index generated from `projects_to`; semantic contradictions are graded by the projections_match_reality rubric item.

decisions.title: DEC-001 Person, User, Actor, Profile, and Subject
decisions.choice: The platform has a canonical `person` for known school people and a broader `agent` concept for non-person actors. Credential subjects and Caliper actors resolve to `person` only when policy and identifiers allow it; otherwise they remain linked external agents.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-002 Class, Course, Context, Group, Course Section, and Organization
decisions.choice: The platform separates `course` from `class`. `course` is the catalog/template. `class` is the scheduled teaching section. LTI contexts and Caliper groups can map to a class, course, or other learning context. Common Cartridge organizations are content outlines, not school organizations or scheduled classes.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-003 Roles
decisions.choice: Store source roles exactly, then map them to platform role families: `learner`, `educator`, `administrator`, `guardian`, `mentor`, `staff`, `tool`, `issuer`, and `unknown`.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-004 Enrollment and Membership
decisions.choice: `enrollment` is the canonical roster participation record. `membership` is a contextual service/event view that may be derived from enrollment or captured from an external system.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-005 Results, Scores, Outcomes, and Grade Events
decisions.choice: The platform uses `line_item` as the gradebook target and `result` as the learner's current gradebook state. LTI AGS Score is an incoming write/update message. LTI AGS Result and OneRoster Result map to current result state. QTI outcomes are assessment-runtime variables projected into results only when a delivery or scoring workflow declares that mapping. Caliper GradeEvent is event history, not the gradebook source of truth.

decisions.title: DEC-006 Standards Alignment
decisions.choice: CASE is the canonical standards graph. Other standards store alignment as source metadata and resolve known targets to CASE item identifiers or URIs.

decisions.title: DEC-007 Identifiers
decisions.choice: Internal records have platform IDs. Standard-shaped endpoints expose the standard-native ID as the primary contract when required. Every external ID is stored in a source identifier crosswalk with source system, identifier type, and object relationship.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-008 Time, School Sessions, and Event Timestamps
decisions.choice: Separate calendar periods, event timestamps, availability windows, and validity periods. Store timestamps in UTC with source timezone/offset when provided. Store OneRoster AcademicSession as the school calendar backbone.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-009 Content, Resources, Packages, and Launch Links
decisions.choice: The platform uses a broad `resource` concept for discoverable learning objects, with subtype-specific records for cartridge resources, QTI packages/items/tests, LTI resource links, and Caliper event entities.

decisions.title: DEC-010 Tenant-Owned Data and Shared Reference Data
decisions.choice: Tenant-owned operational records carry tenant boundaries and row-level policy. Public CASE frameworks, public standards metadata, public certification fixtures, and optionally public item banks live in shared reference namespaces. Tenants adopt, pin, override, or privately extend shared reference data through explicit records.

projections.detail: Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-011 Privacy Surfaces
decisions.choice: Generated docs may describe every field and privacy class because they are schema documentation, not records. Live PostgREST may expose tenant-owned `public`, `operational`, `directory`, and `education_record` records only through tenant RLS and scope policy. Edge Functions may expose `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, or sensitive operational fields only through caller JWT, purpose, scope, tenant RLS, and audit logging when the read is sensitive. Static `site/api/*.json` mirrors may contain only synthetic demo records from the seeded `.test` tenant and may include `public`, `operational`, and `directory` fields such as demo email; they may not contain real tenant data, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive_pii`, or secret/system values.

decisions.title: DEC-012 Runtime Coverage Per Spec
decisions.choice: OneRoster core is runtime-backed now through local SQLite, hosted Supabase PostgREST, RLS, and live tests. QTI and CASE are doc-only/generated dictionary projections until runtime slices are built. Caliper, LTI, Security Framework, and Data Privacy have partial runtime backing through authenticated Edge Function receipt/governance paths, but not full standard conformance flows. Full runtime for QTI import, CASE search, Caliper raw-event projection, LTI Advantage services, production OAuth, and privacy workflows remains future work with target dates in the decision/pending work queue.

decisions.title: DEC-013 Audit Response Truth
decisions.choice: Audited Edge Functions must use truthful responses when they include an `audit` block. If the function cannot read the written `audit_log` rows back under the caller's JWT and request identifier, it must omit the audit block or return an explicit error; it must not hard-code `logged` counts or field lists.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-014 Static Mirror Policy
decisions.choice: Static mirrors are allowed as documentation fixtures for GitHub Pages and offline inspection. They are not the canonical runtime contract; live Supabase REST and Edge Functions are the runtime contract. Mirrors must be generated from the same synthetic demo seed as the local demo, must carry source metadata, and may only include privacy classes allowed by `DEC-011-privacy-surfaces`.

decisions.title: DEC-015 Service-Role Policy
decisions.choice: Request-scoped runtime code and Edge Functions must pass the caller's `Authorization` bearer token to Supabase so RLS applies. Service-role access is allowed only for allowlisted admin or test fixture operations in `docs/admin-operations.md`, with a named operation ID, caller, why-user-JWT-insufficient reason, guardrails, and recent review date. Tests may use service role to create temporary Auth users, but not to read tenant data or satisfy the assertion under test.

decisions.title: DEC-016 Dictionary Generation Direction
decisions.choice: `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` is upstream. `dictionary/*.v1.json` files are generated projections; hand-editing a per-spec projection is a build error once the generator lands.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-017 Canonical Field Reconciliation
decisions.choice: Every overlapping spec field carries `canonical_field_id` pointing at the shared dictionary. Spec-native names remain for conformance; cross-spec app code uses the canonical field identity.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-018 Global Enum Crosswalks
decisions.choice: Allowed values are global dictionary objects. Each enum field references `shared_enum_id`; the shared enum maps every spec-native value to and from a canonical value.

projections.detail: Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-019 Closed Privacy Classes
decisions.choice: Closed keys are `public`, `operational`, `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive`, `credential`, `privacy_governance`, and `system`. `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents` are invalid.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-020 Relational Graph Migrations
decisions.choice: Shared dictionary objects own source field, target object, target field, cardinality, ownership, and delete behavior. Supabase migrations are generated from that graph.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
